_comment: World B usage-aware cascade. EXTENDS the merged kind→executor routing (promptcraft/references/manifest-schema.md). A chunk's executor intent maps to a class here; cascade-dispatch.sh walks that class's role ladder, resolves roles per host via harness-profile.json, checks live headroom (usage-probe.sh) + the quality_floor, dispatches the best available rung, and on a cap error fires the Airlift Tier-1 checkpoint and DESCENDS — generalizing the orchestrator's single 'Codex unavailable -> Claude' fallback into a full ladder. DeepSeek mechanical-agent routing in dm-review is unaffected.

class_from_kind._comment: kind (manifest) -> cascade class. Mirrors the merged Executor Mapping: logic/config default to the codex ladder, ui/integration to the claude ladder.

quality_rank._comment: Global model->score; enforces quality_floor regardless of which role surfaced the model. Higher = better.

codex._comment: Primary executor = Codex (kind logic/config). Sub first; when Codex caps, descend to a cheap open model via OpenRouter BEFORE falling all the way to Claude — this is the key cost win and the generalization of the merged single fallback.

claude._comment: Primary executor = Claude (kind ui/integration — needs visual/Live Wires judgment). Claude first; cross to a frontier API model only when the Max plan is capped. Never drops to a cheap model — quality floor 80 protects ui/integration work.

policy._comment_headroom: Treat a sub/native rail as exhausted once probed remaining quota drops below this — hand off BEFORE the hard wall (mirrors Airlift's early-warning trip).
policy._comment_reentry: When an exhausted sub rail's resets_at passes it re-enters at its original ladder position (flat-rate and free again — prefer quality).
policy.privacy: For cheap_api/frontier_api wrapper rows, pin a no-logging Western provider (Fireworks/Together) for any model whose first-party API trains on data (e.g. DeepSeek). Enforced per-call via OPENROUTER_ZDR=1 in openrouter-wrapper.sh.
policy._comment_headroom_unknown: usage-probe state 'unknown' is treated as HAS-headroom (fail-open): a stale/unverified parser prefers dispatch + reactive cap detection over a false 'capped' that needlessly skips a healthy rail. The codex rail has a reactive cap detector (usage-limit string match) in cascade-dispatch.sh; the claude native rail relies on the orchestrator's own cap handling.
policy._comment_agentic_seam: One-shot vs agentic: wrapper rungs (cheap_api/frontier_api) return single-turn text. For one-shot kinds (config/doc) the orchestrator writes that text -- the cheap rung is a genuine cost win. For agentic kinds (logic/ui/integration) the orchestrator's RC-0 rule discards wrapper text and re-dispatches to Claude, so a capped agentic chunk may execute ONE wrapper call that is then discarded. This is the intentional Phase-B seam: the wrapper rung stays in the ladder so a future agentic openrouter-exec runner (Phase B) plugs in at the orchestrator without re-ordering the ladder. Until Phase B, that one discarded call is the accepted cost; teaching the engine to skip wrapper rungs for agentic kinds is the alternative if the cost matters.
